resources.name: Kong Gateway Bootcamp
resources.description: Complete test collection for Kong Gateway Bootcamp - services, routes, and plugin demos.

resources.name: 01 - Services & Routes
resources.description: Basic connectivity tests for all 3 backend services

resources.name: GET httpbun/headers
GET httpbun/ip
GET httpbun/anything
GET httpbun/get
POST httpbun/post
02 - Rate Limiting
resources.description: Demonstrates rate limiting (5 req/min on httpbin-service)

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (rate limited)
GET httpbun/get (no rate limit)
03 - Proxy Cache
resources.description: Demonstrates response caching (30s TTL on httpbin-service GET requests)

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (1st call - Cache Miss)
GET httpbun/get (2nd call - Cache Hit)
POST httpbun/post (not cached)
04 - Upstream / Load Balancing
resources.description: Demonstrates round-robin load balancing across httpbin.org + httpbun.com

resources.name: GET /lb (call 1 - observe backend)
GET /lb (call 2 - observe different backend)
GET /lb (call 3 - round robin continues)
05 - Key Auth
resources.description: Demonstrates API key authentication on httpbin-service

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (no key - 401)
GET httpbun/get (wrong key - 401)
GET httpbun/get (key in header - 200)
GET httpbun/get (key in query - 200)
GET httpbun/get (test-user key - 200)
06 - JWT Auth
resources.description: Demonstrates JWT authentication on httpbun-service

resources.name: 07 - Consumers
resources.description:
Multiple consumers with API keys. Requires 05 - Key Auth applied first:
  deck gateway apply deck/05-key-auth.yaml
  deck gateway apply deck/07-consumers.yaml

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (no token - 401)
GET httpbun/get (with JWT - 200)
resources.description:
Set jwt_token in environment first. Replace <REPLACE-WITH-RANDOM-SECRET> with the value you wrote into deck/06-jwt-auth.yaml:
python3 -c "import jwt,time; print(jwt.encode({'iss':'my-jwt-issuer','exp':int(time.time())+3600},'<REPLACE-WITH-RANDOM-SECRET>',algorithm='HS256'))"

resources.name: 08 - CORS
resources.description: Demonstrates Cross-Origin Resource Sharing headers

resources.name: GET with Origin header
OPTIONS preflight request
GET with disallowed Origin
09 - IP Restriction
resources.description: Demonstrates IP-based access control on httpbin-service

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (local IP - allowed)
GET httpbun/get (with X-Forwarded-For)
resources.description: Note: X-Forwarded-For spoofing depends on trusted_ips config

resources.name: 10 - Correlation ID
resources.description: Demonstrates request tracing with Correlation ID

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (auto-generated ID)
GET httpbun/headers (verify upstream received ID)
GET httpbun/get (send own Correlation ID)
11 - Request Transformer
resources.description: Demonstrates adding/modifying request headers and query params before upstream

resources.name: GET httpbun/headers (see added headers)
GET httpbun/get (see added query params)
12 - Response Transformer
resources.description: Demonstrates adding/removing response headers

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (check modified response headers)
13 - HTTP Log
resources.description: Demonstrates logging requests to external HTTP endpoint. Start log receiver first!

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (triggers log)
resources.description:
Start log receiver first:
python3 -c "from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler; import json
class H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_POST(self):
        data = self.rfile.read(int(self.headers['Content-Length']))
        print(json.dumps(json.loads(data), indent=2))
        self.send_response(200); self.end_headers()
HTTPServer(('0.0.0.0', 9999), H).serve_forever()"

resources.name: POST httpbun/post (triggers log with body)
14 - Consumer Groups & ACL
resources.description: Demonstrates access control lists and tiered rate limiting

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (no key - 401)
GET httpbun/get (premium user - 200, high limit)
GET httpbun/get (standard user - 200, low limit)
GET httpbun/get (trial user - 403 Forbidden)
15 - Kong Identity (M2M)
resources.description: Konnect-native OAuth2 (client_credentials). Create an Auth Server + Client in Konnect → Identity, then set kong_identity_* env vars. Apply deck/15-kong-identity.yaml (httpbin-service).

resources.name: POST get token (Kong Identity, client_credentials)
resources.description: Mints an M2M token from the Kong Identity auth server and stores it in the kong_identity_token env var. Confirm the exact token path from <issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration.

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (no token - 401)
GET httpbun/get (with bearer - 200)
resources.description: Run 'POST get token' first to populate kong_identity_token.

resources.name: 16 - Keycloak OIDC
resources.description: OpenID Connect via the shared Keycloak (realm bootcamp). Start it: cd ../keycloak && docker compose up -d. Add 127.0.0.1 host.docker.internal to /etc/hosts so the issuer matches. Apply deck/16-oidc-keycloak.yaml (httpbun-service).

resources.name: POST get token (alice, password grant)
resources.description: Mints alice's access token from Keycloak and stores it in keycloak_token. Mint against host.docker.internal so the iss matches the issuer Kong validates.

resources.name: GET httpbun/get (no token - 401)
GET httpbun/get (with bearer - 200)
resources.description: Run 'POST get token' first to populate keycloak_token.

resources.name: POST introspect token (active: true)
resources.description: RFC 7662 introspection. Returns {active:true,...} for a live token; after the user logs out in Keycloak it returns active:false.

resources.name: 17 - Upstream OAuth
resources.description: Kong is the OAuth client: it fetches an M2M token (kong-m2m) and injects it on the UPSTREAM call. Apply deck/17-upstream-oauth.yaml (httpbin-service). The client below sends NO auth.

resources.name: GET httpbun/headers (see Kong-injected Bearer)
resources.description: The client sends no Authorization header. The echoed response should show an Authorization: Bearer token that Kong obtained from Keycloak using kong-m2m.
